task_updates.executor_notes:
Wrote the five Phase 1 migration files with a `20260423` timestamp sequence: `20260423000000_assets_discord_import_cols.sql`, `20260423000001_asset_comments.sql`, `20260423000002_media_import_jobs.sql`, `20260423000003_internal_secrets_bootstrap.sql`, and `20260423000004_discord_messages_resource_op_idx.sql`. The assets migration adds `source`, `discord_guild_id`, `discord_channel_id`, `discord_thread_id`, `imported_at`, `last_synced_at`, `reactions_reached_threshold_at`, and `is_hidden`, plus `assets_discord_thread_id_unique`, `assets_source_idx`, and partial `assets_is_hidden_idx`. The asset-comments migration creates `asset_comments` with `discord_guild_id BIGINT NOT NULL` per T1's zero-null staging check, creates `asset_comment_media` with `is_deleted`, adds `asset_media.is_deleted`, and adds public-read RLS policies filtered on `is_deleted = FALSE`. The media-import-jobs migration makes `media.classification` accept `'discord-comment'` whether the column is backed by an enum or an existing CHECK, then creates `media_import_jobs` with the partial unique attachment index and the `(status, locked_until)` claim index and enables RLS with no policies. The internal-secrets migration creates `internal.secrets`, enables RLS with no policies, and creates `internal.get_service_role_key()` as `SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = ''` with execute granted only to `postgres`. The final migration creates the partial composite `discord_messages_resource_op_idx` on `(channel_id, reaction_count DESC)` where `message_id = thread_id` and `is_deleted = FALSE`.

Verification in this batch was file-level rather than database-level: I re-opened each written migration, confirmed all five files exist under `supabase/migrations/`, and checked their names, line counts, and git status. I also re-ran the batch baseline command after the edits. In this environment, both the pre-edit and post-edit runs of `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` fail during collection with the same 16 test-module errors, so there is no observed regression delta attributable to these migration-file changes.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. All five `20260423` migration files are present under `supabase/migrations/` and were re-opened after writing. The assets migration adds `discord_guild_id` as nullable alongside the other Discord-import columns and indexes. The asset-comments migration sets `asset_comments.discord_guild_id BIGINT NOT NULL` per T1's zero-null staging result, adds `is_deleted` to both `asset_comment_media` and `asset_media`, and adds public-read RLS on the new tables filtered to `is_deleted = FALSE`. The media-import-jobs migration creates both required indexes and leaves RLS enabled with no policies. The internal-secrets migration creates `internal.secrets` plus `internal.get_service_role_key()` as a `SECURITY DEFINER` accessor. The final migration creates the partial composite `discord_messages_resource_op_idx`.
